summary: Backend testing complete for WhatsApp Connection Monitor iteration 2. 28/28 pytest tests passed (100%) covering all new features: pairing-mode toggle, pairing-code generation, /qr {state,qr,code,mode} shape, monitor labels (POST + PATCH success + 404), /events filters (phone, event_type), /events/export.csv (header + filters + content-type), settings daily_summary fields + persistence, email_enabled without recipient -> 400, daily_summary_hour clamp to 23, send-summary-now (disabled 400 + enabled 200 success path), internal webhook X-Internal-Secret auth (no header 401, wrong 401, valid client_state + presence). Regression suite (status, qr, monitors CRUD, events CRUD, settings, websocket hello + broadcast) all green.

minor.issue: No validation on `mode` query param — any string is forwarded to Node sidecar. Node accepts only 'qr' or 'code' but FastAPI should validate (e.g. Literal['qr','code']) and return 400 on invalid values.
Still public with no auth — wipes WA session and all monitors. Same concern as iteration 1.

When Resend send fails (e.g. unverified recipient), endpoint returns 400 with the same generic 'summary not sent' message as the disabled path — caller cannot distinguish 'misconfig' vs 'transient send failure'. Consider returning 502 with the resend error detail for the latter.

PATCH with empty body { } returns 400 'no fields to update' (good), but PATCH with {label: ''} silently sets label to None (treats empty string as clear). Behaviour is reasonable but undocumented — confirm intent.

action_items: Optional: validate `mode` param on POST /api/whatsapp/pairing-mode using Literal['qr','code'].
Optional (carryover from iter 1): gate /api/whatsapp/logout behind some auth.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is ~700 lines and growing — scheduler (daily_summary_loop), models, helpers, and routes could be split into separate modules.
daily_summary_loop catches all exceptions but sleeps 60s on success path only — on a tight error loop it does NOT sleep, potentially busy-looping. Add a sleep in the except branch as well.

send_daily_summary_email logs 'last_summary_sent_at' but uses iso(now_utc()) string while reading it back via datetime.fromisoformat — works because Python 3.11 supports 'Z' but fragile across versions.
Internal secret check in verify_internal_secret silently passes if INTERNAL_API_SECRET env var is empty — fail-closed posture would be safer in production.

PATCH /api/monitors broadcasts a monitor_updated message but the embedded `added_at` is being re-iso'd from a possibly-None value (guarded), and last_seen is not converted — minor inconsistency vs add_monitor broadcast payload.
seed_data_creation: Tests seed monitors directly into Mongo (TEST_PHONE_2=33687654321, 33611111111) for PATCH and send-summary-now flows since WA client is unpaired; teardown removes them. Events are seeded via /api/internal/event using INTERNAL_API_SECRET and cleared in fixture teardown.
context_for_next_testing_agent: WA client is in 'qr' state by default. Node sidecar exposes /pairing-mode and /pairing-code. Pairing-code generation succeeded (whatsapp-web.js requestPairingCode returned a code). To exercise PATCH /monitors and send-summary-now success paths, seed monitors directly via Mongo (MONGO_URL/DB_NAME from /app/backend/.env). Resend sends to delivered@resend.dev succeeded in this run. Internal webhook now requires X-Internal-Secret header — old test scripts that POST /api/internal/event without it will 401.
